output: Batch 3 executed for T3, T4, and T5. The indicator path now carries both `isNewTrackTop` and `trackId`, the top drop label overlay is absolutely positioned so it no longer affects row layout, and internal clip drags now pass top-insert intent through to `createTrackAndMoveClip`, which inserts at track index `0` when requested. Verification was code-level for this batch; the TypeScript compile check remains queued for T6.

Did not run `npx tsc --noEmit` in this batch because compilation is explicitly scoped to T6.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified `DropIndicatorPosition` now declares both `isNewTrackTop?: boolean` and `trackId?: string` in `DropIndicator.tsx`. Verified `toIndicatorPosition()` in `useDragCoordinator.ts` forwards both fields from `DropPosition`. Verified the label generation still reads `position?.isNewTrackTop` and updated the effect dependency list to include that field so the top/bottom label stays correct while dragging.

Verified the top indicator div in `TimelineCanvas.tsx` now uses `absolute top-0 left-0 right-0 z-10` and retains `pointer-events-none`. Verified its parent container remains `className="relative"`, so the label will overlay the timeline instead of participating in normal flow and shifting row hit-test geometry.

Verified `createTrackAndMoveClip` now accepts `insertAtTop?: boolean` in both the hook result type and callback implementation. When `insertAtTop` is true it now calls `addTrack(current.resolvedConfig, kind, 0)`. Verified `useClipDrag.ts` forwards `dropPosition.isNewTrackTop` as the 4th argument at the single-clip cross-track drop site. Also verified `addTrack()` in `editor-utils.ts` already supports indexed insertion via `splice`, so index `0` insertion is valid.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `DropIndicatorPosition` now declares both `isNewTrackTop` and `trackId`, `toIndicatorPosition()` forwards both fields, and the new-track label path still derives `top` vs `bottom` from `position?.isNewTrackTop`. I also added `position?.isNewTrackTop` to the effect dependencies so that label remains accurate during drag updates.

Yes. The top indicator div now has `pointer-events-none absolute top-0 left-0 right-0 z-10`, and its containing timeline section is already `relative`. That makes it an overlay rather than a normal-flow element, so it will not push track rows down. This was verified from the rendered class/style code, not via browser interaction.

Yes. `createTrackAndMoveClip` now accepts `insertAtTop` and passes `0` to `addTrack()` when true, otherwise `undefined`. `useClipDrag` forwards `dropPosition.isNewTrackTop` as the 4th argument. I also verified `addTrack()` already supports indexed insertion with `splice`, so the top-insert path is valid.
